TestCase z33Change(TestCase t1) {
    TestCase follow_case = t1;
    // --- Memory handling code generated based on the rule ---
    if (t1.n2 > 0) {
        double* follow_case_b = (double*)malloc(t1.n2 * sizeof(double));
        if (follow_case_b != NULL) {
            memcpy(follow_case_b, t1.b, t1.n2 * sizeof(double));
            follow_case.b = follow_case_b;
        } else {
            // malloc failed, return original follow_case without modification
            return follow_case;
        }
    } else {
        // If n2 is 0 or negative, no allocation needed for b
        return follow_case;
    }
    // ------------------------------------

    // Defensive code generated based on rule (a) to prevent errors when n2 < 1
    if (follow_case.n2 < 1) {
        // No elements in b, return replica directly
        return follow_case;
    }

    // Specific operation code generated based on the rule
    // Find all indices where b[i] < 1
    int count = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < follow_case.n2; i++) {
        if (follow_case.b[i] < 1.0) {
            count++;
        }
    }
    if (count == 0) {
        // No element less than 1, nothing to do
        return follow_case;
    }

    // Collect indices of elements less than 1
    int* indices = (int*)malloc(count * sizeof(int));
    if (indices == NULL) {
        // malloc failed, return follow_case unchanged
        return follow_case;
    }
    int idx = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < follow_case.n2; i++) {
        if (follow_case.b[i] < 1.0) {
            indices[idx++] = i;
        }
    }
    // Select a random position from indices
    // Use rand() % count; no srand here as per instruction
    int random_pos = indices[rand() % count];
    follow_case.b[random_pos] = 1.0;

    free(indices);

    return follow_case;
}
